forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KXJWGS1DPP92870X79GPX96T
+- 01KXJWH0ZGPEAKRDWN520P15XY
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8580'
+title: AtomicWriter + write file operation
+---
+## What
+Per plan.md Â§3 op table row 2. Create `Sources/FileTool/AtomicWriter.swift` and `Sources/FileTool/Operations/WriteFile.swift`:
+- `AtomicWriter`: write to `{path}.tmp.{uuid}` in the **same directory** â†’ `rename` onto target; single cleanup path removes temp on any failure (write or rename); creates parent directories; re-applies original permission bits when overwriting; owns encoding/line-ending detection+re-encode hooks (consumed fully by the edit task)
+- `WriteFile` op: params `filePath` (req), `content` (req, â‰¤ 10 MiB â†’ corrective over cap). Unconditional clobber (no freshness check â€” parity). Output `WriteResult: Encodable { path, bytesWritten, hash, taggedContent, diagnostics? }` with `diagnostics` left nil (bridge task wires it)
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Interrupted/failed write leaves target untouched and no `.tmp.*` files behind (incl. rename-failure: target is a directory)
+- [ ] Overwriting a 0755 file keeps it 0755
+- [ ] `taggedContent` anchors are lift-able: a chained edit resolves one without an intervening read (assert once edit op lands)
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/WriteFileTests.swift`: new/overwrite/parent creation; blank path corrective; content exactly over 10 MiB rejected, at cap accepted; readonly target corrective; cleanup on write-failure and rename-failure; no temp files remain (directory scan); unicode/empty content; envelope fields (bytesWritten, hash matches re-read token)
+- [ ] Run `swift test --filter WriteFileTests` â€” expect: green
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` â€” write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,13 +11,15 @@
 - `AtomicWriter`: write to `{path}.tmp.{uuid}` in the **same directory** â†’ `rename` onto target; single cleanup path removes temp on any failure (write or rename); creates parent directories; re-applies original permission bits when overwriting; owns encoding/line-ending detection+re-encode hooks (consumed fully by the edit task)
 - `WriteFile` op: params `filePath` (req), `content` (req, â‰¤ 10 MiB â†’ corrective over cap). Unconditional clobber (no freshness check â€” parity). Output `WriteResult: Encodable { path, bytesWritten, hash, taggedContent, diagnostics? }` with `diagnostics` left nil (bridge task wires it)

+(Anchor-chaining into a subsequent edit is verified by the edit-file task, which owns that acceptance criterion.)
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] Interrupted/failed write leaves target untouched and no `.tmp.*` files behind (incl. rename-failure: target is a directory)
 - [ ] Overwriting a 0755 file keeps it 0755
-- [ ] `taggedContent` anchors are lift-able: a chained edit resolves one without an intervening read (assert once edit op lands)
+- [ ] `taggedContent` anchors and `hash` in the envelope match a subsequent `read file` of the same path

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/WriteFileTests.swift`: new/overwrite/parent creation; blank path corrective; content exactly over 10 MiB rejected, at cap accepted; readonly target corrective; cleanup on write-failure and rename-failure; no temp files remain (directory scan); unicode/empty content; envelope fields (bytesWritten, hash matches re-read token)
+- [ ] `Tests/FileToolTests/WriteFileTests.swift`: new/overwrite/parent creation; blank path corrective; content exactly over 10 MiB rejected, at cap accepted; readonly target corrective; cleanup on write-failure and rename-failure; no temp files remain (directory scan); unicode/empty content; envelope fields (bytesWritten, hash matches re-read token, taggedContent equals read-back tagging)
 - [ ] Run `swift test --filter WriteFileTests` â€” expect: green

 ## Workflow

+  id: 01kxkrwwev11apqbrxhngchmat
+  text: 'Research done. Key findings before coding:\n- Reuse: Hashline.wholeFileHash(bytes:) for hash, Hashline.tag(lines:startingAtLine:1)+splitLines for taggedContent (identical computation to ReadFile''s whole-file hashline path, so a subsequent read matches). PathGuard.validate(path, .write) for validation + corrective (return-don''t-throw via WriteOutput enum mirroring ReadOutput).\n- CONSTRAINT: PathGuard (completed dep) rejects a write whose immediate parent is missing (validatePath ENOENT->parentDirectoryMissing AND checkWritePermission), so deep parent-creation cannot occur through the op â€” it returns the PathGuard corrective (matches upstream ''write: parent exists''). AtomicWriter still OWNS parent creation as a reusable primitive; unit-tested at the AtomicWriter level (AtomicWriterTests), not reachable via the op path.\n- FileDiagnostics type doesn''t exist yet (bridge task 1crx7kf creates it and wires WriteResult.diagnostics). Adding a minimal STUB FileDiagnostics (Encodable&Sendable), mirroring the existing DiagnosticsBridge stub precedent, so WriteResult carries diagnostics=nil now.\n- readOnly context flag: deferring enforcement to the makeReadOnly dispatch task (plan Â§''read/glob/grep only''); WriteFile op stays on the spec''d cases.\n- Cleanup tests through the op: rename-failure via target-is-a-directory (PathGuard passes an existing dir for .write); write-failure via read-only parent dir. Both assert no .tmp.* remain via directory scan.'

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -8,6 +8,10 @@
   id: 01kxkrwwev11apqbrxhngchmat
   text: 'Research done. Key findings before coding:
- Reuse: Hashline.wholeFileHash(bytes:) for hash, Hashline.tag(lines:startingAtLine:1)+splitLines for taggedContent (identical computation to ReadFile''s whole-file hashline path, so a subsequent read matches). PathGuard.validate(path, .write) for validation + corrective (return-don''t-throw via WriteOutput enum mirroring ReadOutput).
- CONSTRAINT: PathGuard (completed dep) rejects a write whose immediate parent is missing (validatePath ENOENT->parentDirectoryMissing AND checkWritePermission), so deep parent-creation cannot occur through the op â€” it returns the PathGuard corrective (matches upstream ''write: parent exists''). AtomicWriter still OWNS parent creation as a reusable primitive; unit-tested at the AtomicWriter level (AtomicWriterTests), not reachable via the op path.
- FileDiagnostics type doesn''t exist yet (bridge task 1crx7kf creates it and wires WriteResult.diagnostics). Adding a minimal STUB FileDiagnostics (Encodable&Sendable), mirroring the existing DiagnosticsBridge stub precedent, so WriteResult carries diagnostics=nil now.
- readOnly context flag: deferring enforcement to the makeReadOnly dispatch task (plan Â§''read/glob/grep only''); WriteFile op stays on the spec''d cases.
- Cleanup tests through the op: rename-failure via target-is-a-directory (PathGuard passes an existing dir for .write); write-failure via read-only parent dir. Both assert no .tmp.* remain via directory scan.'
   timestamp: 2026-07-15T20:53:36.347184+00:00
+- actor: claude-code
+  id: 01kxkscgvyvqmqvhytce53eern
+  text: 'Implemented (TDD: tests written first, watched RED = types missing, then GREEN).

Files:
- Sources/FileTool/AtomicWriter.swift â€” enum AtomicWriter. write(_:to:): capture original perms (stat) BEFORE write; createParentDirectory (intermediates); write bytes to sibling temp {path}.tmp.{UUID} in the SAME dir; re-apply original perm bits to the temp; POSIX rename temp onto target (atomic replace). ONE cleanup path: a single do/catch that `try? removeItem(temp)` on ANY throw (write/chmod/rename) then rethrows; on success the rename consumes the temp so nothing leaks. Also owns the edit-task hooks: TextEncoding (utf-8 / utf-8 bom) decode(_:) (BOM sniff + UTF-8 fallback, nil on binary) + encode(_:as:) (round-trips), and LineEnding (lf/crlf/cr/mixed) detectLineEnding(in:) reusing Hashline.splitLines. AtomicWriteError for errno failures.
- Sources/FileTool/Operations/WriteFile.swift â€” @Generable @Operation(verb write, noun file). Params filePath, content. Return-don''t-throw WriteOutput enum (content/corrective) mirroring ReadOutput exactly. Pipeline: content-size cap (UTF-8 bytes > 10 MiB via named constants maximumContentMebibytes*bytesPerMebibyte) -> corrective; PathGuard.validate(.write) -> corrective; AtomicWriter.write in do/catch -> corrective. Unconditional clobber (no freshness/hash param). WriteResult{path,bytesWritten,hash,taggedContent,diagnostics=nil}; hash=Hashline.wholeFileHash, taggedContent=Hashline.tag+splitLines (identical to ReadFile whole-file hashline path).
- Sources/FileTool/FileDiagnostics.swift â€” empty STUB (Encodable&Sendable) so WriteResult carries diagnostics=nil now; bridge task 1crx7kf fleshes it out (mirrors DiagnosticsBridge stub precedent).

Atomicity/cleanup/permissions: temp+rename is atomic on one filesystem; single catch removes temp on every failure path; perms captured pre-write and re-applied to temp pre-rename so 0755 overwrite stays 0755 (new files skip chmod).

Tests (all green): Tests/FileToolTests/WriteFileTests.swift (op-level: new, overwrite, blank-path, over-cap rejected + no file, at-cap accepted, readonly-target corrective+untouched, rename-failure via target-is-dir + directory scan no .tmp.*, write-failure via read-only parent dir + scan, unicode, empty, 0755 preservation, bytesWritten=UTF-8 bytes, hash==re-read token, taggedContent==read-back lines). Tests/FileToolTests/AtomicWriterTests.swift (parent creation â€” unreachable via op since PathGuard requires an existing parent, so unit-tested here; line-ending lf/crlf/cr/mixed/none; encoding decode+round-trip+binary reject).

Verification: `swift test --filter ''WriteFileTests|AtomicWriterTests''` = 25 pass; full `swift test` = 88 tests/6 suites + 1 integration test, all pass; no build warnings. Adversarial double-check: PASS (no correctness/atomicity/cleanup/convention findings).

Deliberate deferrals (noted): parent-creation not reachable through the op (PathGuard gate); readOnly-context enforcement deferred to the makeReadOnly dispatch task. Task left in doing for /review.'
+  timestamp: 2026-07-15T21:02:08.766757+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KXJWGS1DPP92870X79GPX96T
 - 01KXJWH0ZGPEAKRDWN520P15XY
